alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google in commercial operations and transformation, with experience in process design, requirements logic, UAT, and rollout execution
alignments.job_requirement: Administration of Checkout's Identity platform, including Okta and joiner, mover leaver process; Develop and maintain user account provisioning and deprovisioning processes

alignments.candidate_evidence: Demonstrated cross-functional stakeholder management and transformation program leadership at Google, with requirements gathering and design translation skills
alignments.job_requirement: Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather IAM requirements and translate them into technical designs; Collaborate with internal stakeholders to understand IAM needs

alignments.candidate_evidence: Experience operating within regulated environments and compliance frameworks through Google commercial operations scaling
alignments.job_requirement: Work closely with InfoSec team to ensure seamless execution of IAM-related tasks and controls for audits such as PCI DSS, ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type II

gaps.gap: No hands-on Okta experience (Identity Engine, Org-to-Org, Workflows, Device Access)
gaps.mitigation: Core tool expertise can be acquired in months with on-the-job training and self-study, but requires immediate ramp time

gaps.gap: No IAM protocol expertise (SAML, OIDC/OAuth, SCIM integration)
gaps.mitigation: Technical depth in identity protocols requires specialized study; candidate's background is in business operations, not identity engineering

gaps.gap: No hands-on cloud IAM administration (AWS IAM, GCP IAM)
gaps.mitigation: Candidate has commercial operations and transformation experience, not platform identity and access management

gaps.gap: Python basic and actively learning; no demonstrated Node.js or PowerShell scripting for automation at scale
gaps.mitigation: Role requires 'proficiency in scripting and automation' as a core responsibility; candidate's coding is foundational

gaps.gap: No infrastructure-as-code or policy-as-code (Terraform, CI/CD) experience
gaps.mitigation: These are specialized technical skills; candidate's engineering depth does not match the stated requirement

gaps.gap: IT/IAM or infrastructure engineering background absent; candidate is strategy and operations-focused
gaps.mitigation: Role requires deep hands-on IAM engineering; candidate's background is in business transformation and commercial operations, a fundamentally different domain

hard_blockers.evidence: Hands-on experience with Okta, including Okta Identity Engine, Okta Org-to-Org, Okta Workflows, and Okta Device Access; Strong background in integrating identity applications using protocols such as SAML, OIDC/OAuth, and SCIM; Proven expertise in managing platform identity and access controls across cloud environments (AWS IAM, GCP IAM); Proficiency in scripting and automation using languages like Node.js, Python, or PowerShell, leveraging config-as-code and policy-as-code principles (Terraform, CI/CD pipelines).

output.level_rationale: Role is mid-level IC engineering (L4) based on hands-on technical accountability for Okta administration, integration, automation, and incident response without stated people management. Title "Senior Engineer" and multi-protocol, multi-platform IAM scope suggest L4–L5 IC depth in a fintech regulated environment; no manager-of-managers scope signals. Specific technology stack (Okta, SAML/OIDC, AWS/GCP IAM, Terraform, CI/CD) and "proficiency in scripting" anchor the role as production engineering, not strategy or operations.
output.summary: This is a hands-on IAM/Okta engineering role, not a strategy, operations, or business transformation position. The candidate's eight-year background in Google commercial operations, process design, and transformation program leadership does not align with production identity engineering, cloud platform administration, IAM protocol integration, or infrastructure-as-code automation. Core hard blockers include no Okta experience, no SAML/OIDC/SCIM protocol expertise, no AWS/GCP IAM administration, and only foundational Python skills—all stated as required. Role family fit is deeply misaligned; recommend skip.
